parse_notes: Fractional coordinates converted to Cartesian using the lattice matrix built from _cell_length_* / _cell_angle_* (Part 2 §4).
pbc set to (true,true,true): a CIF describes a crystal, so full 3-D periodicity is format-defined (Part 3 §3 n.3), not assumed.

Symmetry expansion: 3 declared site(s) expanded to 3 atom(s) by applying the 1 symmetry operation(s) the file declares (Part 3 §3 n.13). Per-site multiplicities: [1, 1, 1]. 0 coincident image(s) were merged as special positions within 0.05 Å. Coordinates generated by an operation were wrapped into the unit cell; declared coordinates are carried as the file spelled them.

occupancy carried as a custom per-atom array: the file declares _atom_site_occupancy and the Canonical Model has no occupancy field, so the column is preserved verbatim in user_metadata.custom_per_atom['cif:occupancy'] rather than modelled (Part 3 §3 n.11). Only the CIF target can write it back.

partial site occupancy: 2 of 3 sites declare an occupancy that is not 1 (unknown values, '?' or '.', count as not-1 — silence is not a claim of fullness). The structure carries a whole atom at every such site, so its composition is the fully-occupied one and does not equal the file's own _chemical_formula_sum. CIF is the only Phase 1 target that writes the column back; converting to any other target discards the distinction.
source values carried a parenthesized standard uncertainty, which was read as the value and whose precision digits are not modelled: ['_cell_length_a', '_cell_length_b', '_cell_length_c']
